plan.summary:
19-step plan, dependency-ordered: seeded-RNG substrate â†’ cell/capacity model â†’ job-board multi-line Order model â†’ stage store â†’ work generators (random demand, truck pooling) â†’ truck/robot brains (landing, cross-dock, quantity, grouping, charging) â†’ bootstrap wiring â†’ tests.

THREE ARCHITECTURAL CONSTRAINTS discovered while reading the code, which the plan is built around:

1. CellType is INDEX-SERIALIZED. warehouse_config.dart:319 writes `'t': type.index` and :330 reads `CellType.values[j['t'] as int]`. `landing`/`outboundStage` MUST be appended at indices 33/34. Inserting them next to the other staging types (the "natural" spot) silently remaps every persisted layout and share-code. Their label/color switches are exhaustive `switch` expressions, so step 2 breaks compilation across the UI until every arm is added â€” that is exactly the "enum/exhaustive-switch churn" outbound_stage.dart:7-9 deferred, and it cannot be deferred again.

2. THE SINGLE-UOM COUPLING IS LOAD-BEARING AND MUST BE CUT ATOMICALLY. sim_bootstrap.dart:58+66 gives every picker one warehouse-wide UOM from `_warehousePickUom`, and outbound_order_generator_brain.dart:91 derives order UOM from the same first-stocked-rack scan. The comment at sim_bootstrap.dart:54-57 states these agree *by construction*. Job.matchesRole (job_board.dart:208) hard-gates on UOM, and claimableFor filters BEFORE the attempts counter increments â€” so a UOM-mismatched job is never claimed, never counted, never failed, and its Order never aborts. Fixing either side alone (random UOM without heterogeneous pickers, or heterogeneous pickers without mixed demand) produces permanently unclaimable jobs â†’ Order pinned open â†’ wipCap blocks all future orders â†’ docked truck holds its bay forever. Steps 7 and 18 must land together.

3. RANDOM QUANTITY IS A LIVE DEADLOCK, NOT A GRADUAL DEGRADATION. pick_robot_brain.dart:136 hardcodes `pickFromRack(..., 1)` and :177 completes the job unconditionally; it never reads job.qtyUnits. Because completeJob passes no progressUnits, job_board.dart:392 does not advance the Order â€” so a 144-unit order does not under-deliver, it WEDGES at 48/144 with no job left to mint the remainder and no watchdog reachable (the job already settled `done`). Step 14 is therefore a hard prerequisite of step 7's random quantity, not a follow-up.

TEST IMPACT: 6 of the 21 currently-passing tests encode behaviour this spec reverses and must be rewritten as part of the step that breaks them â€” regression_putaway_capacity (step 3: asserts the 48â†’2 fall-through the fix removes), p2_unload_chain (step 11: unloads a truck that was never registered), p4_outbound_loop + p4_outbound_loose (steps 7/8: assert 1:1 truck/order and immediate depart), p4_pick + regression_multipicker (step 6: read stage values as bare Strings). Determinism is preserved by threading one injected `Random` (step 1) through every generator, with tests pinning the seed.

DEAD SCAFFOLDING TO WIRE, NOT REBUILD: OrderLine, Order.lines, Order.divert()/divertedUnits, JobKind.rebalance/departTruck/recovery, OutboundOrderGeneratorBrain.orderUnits, kTruckCapacity, PickZoneType/PickZoneDef, and ScoutBrain all exist and are never constructed/read. Several plan steps are wiring these up rather than writing new models. Notably, the three pick areas of spec 5(a)(b)(c) are ALREADY modelled as PickZoneDef (warehouse_config.dart:176-250, painted by the template factory, paintable in the creator) and are merely cosmetic â€” bind them in pick_robot_brain (step 14); do NOT resurrect the looseStaging/caseStaging CellTypes, which would duplicate that model.

steps.title: Seeded RNG substrate (determinism gate for all later randomness)
steps.what: NEW file sim_random.dart: `const int kSimSeed = 20260716;`, a `simSeedProvider`, and a thin `SimRng` wrapping `dart:math` Random(seed) exposing nextIntIn(min,max) / pickWeighted / jitter. Rule to enforce from here on: NO brain may construct its own Random or read a clock â€” the RNG is injected via constructor so a test can pin it. Today `grep -rn 'dart:math|Random' lib/application/` returns ZERO hits, so this establishes the only sanctioned entry point before steps 7/10 introduce randomness. bootstrapSimUnits builds one SimRng from kSimSeed and hands derived child RNGs (seed+roleOffset) to the generator brains, so adding a consumer never shifts another consumer's draw sequence â€” the property the JEPA eval depends on.
steps.risk: None â€” additive, no existing behaviour touched. This step exists specifically so 'random' in steps 7/10 can never become wall-clock random.

steps.title: CellType: append `landing` + `outboundStage` (APPEND-ONLY â€” index-serialized)
steps.what: Append `landing` (33) and `outboundStage` (34) to the END of the CellType enum (warehouse_config.dart:12-48). MUST be appended, never inserted next to the other staging types: toJson writes `'t': type.index` (:319) and fromJson reads `CellType.values[j['t'] as int]` (:330), so a mid-enum insert silently remaps every saved layout and share-code. Add arms to the exhaustive label/color switches (:50-110), `_defaultMaxQty`, isWalkable, and the domain predicates at :156-159 â€” both are robot-enterable buffers, so mirror palletStaging semantics. Add both to the creator palette (:34-35). Template factory paints a landing strip adjacent to the inbound dock and an outboundStage block adjacent to the outbound bays â€” reuse the existing pack-column alignment logic at :444-457 ('Pack station must always sit directly adjacent to the dock bays'), which is why factory layouts get adjacency today. Add readiness checks for both. Keep packStation accepted as a legacy alias for outboundStage so the p4 tests stay green until step 6 retargets the stage.
steps.risk: WIDEST BLAST RADIUS OF ANY STEP, but it fails LOUDLY not silently: the label/color switches are exhaustive switch expressions, so every unhandled arm is a compile error across the UI â€” that is precisely the churn outbound_stage.dart:7-9 deferred ('the distinct cell type lands with pack/load'; pack/load landed, the cell type never did). The only silent-corruption risk is enum ordering â€” append-only is non-negotiable. No test asserts CellType.values.length.

steps.title: Rack capacity in native units + configurable reorder point (unblocks dead 5.2/5.3)
steps.what: Two coupled unit-system bugs. (a) `_defaultMaxQty` (warehouse_config.dart:311-315) returns rackCase=2 / rackLoose=2 while `_belowThreshold` demands room >= kCasesPerPallet(12) / kLoosePerPallet(48) â€” 2 < 12 < 48, so rules 5.2 and 5.3 are UNREACHABLE in every generated warehouse and 100% of pallets fall through to 5.4. Express face capacity in the same native units as the drop (e.g. rackCase >= 24 cases, rackLoose >= 96 loose). (b) Replace the hardcoded `quantity < maxQuantity * 0.5` getter (:302-303) with a `reorderPoint` field (default derived, with a non-zero floor) â€” at capacity 2 the current expression is `quantity < 1.0`, i.e. it only fires at ZERO, so case/loose faces must fully stock out before any inbound is raised. Consolidate the 0.5 constant, which is triplicated (warehouse_config.dart:302, putaway_robot_brain.dart:30, pallet_putaway_controller.dart:39 â€” at capacity 2 a face at qty 1 gives `0.5 < 0.5` = false, so putaway won't top it up either). Also fix warehouse_template_factory.dart:100-101, which seeds case/loose at 30-70% of capacity 2 â†’ qty 1, i.e. one pick from a stockout that the trigger cannot see.
steps.risk: BREAKS regression_putaway_capacity_test.dart â€” it deliberately seeds a loose face at maxQuantity:2 and asserts (:73-76) the too-small loose rack is left UNTOUCHED while the pallet lands in the pallet rack ('no 48â†’2 destruction'). That test encodes the opposite of this fix. Rewrite it to assert the new capacity-aware intent, KEEPING an explicit maxQuantity:2 case to prove the annihilation guard still holds when a face genuinely is too small. Also touches p3_inbound_loop_test (maxQuantity:2, quantity:0 â€” still low, should stay green) and the creator's quantity clamp (warehouse_creator_screen.dart:1398/1410).

steps.title: Putaway: partial drop + neediest-face-first ranking
steps.what: `_belowThreshold` (:248-263) is all-or-nothing (`room >= need`) and first-match on cfg.cells order. Change to fill-what-fits + return the remainder for the next candidate, and rank candidate faces by fillFraction (neediest first) instead of taking the first hit. Also fix the empty-face branch (:256-259), which checks neither SKU nor threshold, so the first empty loose face in scan order pre-empts 5.3/5.4 even when the case area is starving. NOTE: applying kReplenishThreshold to the empty branch is a no-op (an empty face has fillFraction 0, below any positive threshold) â€” the real defect is the missing SKU check and the missing ranking.
steps.risk: Low once step 3 lands. Partial drop is the alternative to (or complement of) step 3's capacity reconciliation; doing both makes 5.2/5.3 robust for hand-authored layouts that still carry small faces.

steps.title: job_board: multi-line Order model, per-line progress, cross-dock job kind
steps.what: Wire the dead scaffolding rather than inventing a model. OrderLine (:68-75) exists but `OrderLine(` is constructed NOWHERE and `Order.lines` is never read. Add `skuId` + `progressUnits` + `status` to OrderLine (rename `id`â†’`lineId`; today's `id` is ambiguously named and cannot vary SKU). Order: keep `skuId` as a derived back-compat getter (`lines.first.skuId`) so the single-SKU inbound path and p4_pick_test.dart:60 stay green, and make `orderedUnits` derive from Î£ lines. Add `Job.lineId`, `Order.assignedTruckId`, `Order.stagedUnits`/`isFullyStaged` (spec-2 grouping predicate â€” `isSatisfied` at :120 is a post-hoc ALL-UNITS-LOADED test, not a pre-load consolidation gate). completeJob (:386-397) attributes progress to the line via `j.lineId` then rolls up to the Order. Assign the never-written `OrderStatus.fulfilling` (:65 â€” declared, read at 2 sites, assigned nowhere) on first progress. Add `JobKind.crossDock` + `Job.divertedFromInboundLineId` for step 13. Keep a `mintOrder(skuId:...)` convenience overload that builds a single line, so the two existing call sites don't churn.
steps.risk: Signature ripple to stock_monitor_brain.dart:54, outbound_order_generator_brain.dart:55, p4_pick_test.dart:60 â€” contained by the back-compat overload. Preserve the zero-padded `_nextId` (:250-252) and the id-sorted claimableFor (:313): both are deliberate FIFO-determinism guarantees for the JEPA eval. Also preserve the sweepTerminal idem-prefix prune (:444-452), whose `'$oid:'` scheme the new per-line idemKeys must keep matching.

steps.title: outbound_stage: carry order identity, retarget to CellType.outboundStage
steps.what: State becomes `Map<String, StagedItem>` where StagedItem = {skuId, orderId, lineId, units} â€” today it is `Map<String,String>` cellKeyâ†’skuId (:14-23), so NOTHING in the codebase can answer 'what is staged for order X', which makes spec-2 grouping unrepresentable rather than merely unimplemented. Add `cellsFor(orderId)` and `stagedUnits(orderId)`. Thread orderId/lineId through ActionApplier.stageOutbound (:136-139) and takeFromStage (:144-151) â€” preserve takeFromStage's `false`-on-empty contract (review HT-1, the phantom-load guard). Retarget the stage from packStation to CellType.outboundStage (step 2), keeping packStation as a fallback so unmigrated layouts still run. Delete the now-stale reuse note at outbound_stage.dart:7-9.
steps.risk: BREAKS p4_pick_test.dart:86 and regression_multipicker_test.dart:74, which read stage values as bare Strings. Mechanical fix (`.values.map((e) => e.skuId)`). p4_outbound_loop_test's `.isEmpty` check on the map still works.

steps.title: Outbound generator: seeded random SKU/UOM/qty/timing + multi-line explosion
steps.what: Rewrite perceiveAndDecide (:33-77) â€” this single method is spec point 1 in its entirety. (a) SKU: sample via injected SimRng from the SKU CATALOG `kAllSkuIds` (warehouse_template_factory.dart:34, currently never imported by any brain), NOT from `_firstStocked` (:87-95) which scans live rack contents â€” demand must be able to request an out-of-stock SKU, which is the very trigger spec 3 needs. Bound simultaneous zero-stock orders. (b) UOM: sample per line, independent of rack type â€” today `rackUomOf(c.type)` (:91) makes demand UOM a property of the STORAGE LAYOUT, so a rackLoose-painted warehouse (the creator default) emits loose orders forever. (c) Quantity: random multiple of the line's UOM â€” today `units = stocked.uom.looseUnits` (:53) is always exactly 1/4/48. Delete the dead `orderUnits` field (:20,:28 â€” declared, never read; a knob wired to nothing is worse than no knob). (d) Timing: add `_nextEmitAtTick = tick + interval Â± jitter` from the RNG; `ctx.tick` is currently read ONLY to stamp createdTick (:59) and never gates emission. (e) Explode one pickToStage Job per line with idemKey `'${order.id}:L$i:0'` â€” the hardcoded `:L0:0` (:67) is vestigial for lines that never existed. (f) Raise wipCap (:19) from 1 to ~3-4. (g) Stop the 1:1 truck spawn (:70-76) â†’ step 8.
steps.risk: MUST LAND WITH STEP 18 (see summary constraint 2) and MUST NOT LAND BEFORE STEP 14 (constraint 3). Random UOM without heterogeneous pickers mints jobs no picker can claim, which are filtered out by claimableFor BEFORE the attempts counter can fire the releaseOrFail watchdog â†’ permanent wedge. Random quantity without step 14's qty loop deadlocks at 48/144. BREAKS p4_outbound_loop_test and p4_outbound_loose_test (both assume one deterministic order); give the brain a pinned-seed + single-line demandProfile param so those tests pin exact behaviour. Add a determinism test: same seed â†’ byte-identical order stream.

steps.title: Outbound truck: multi-order manifest, capacity, depart-when-full
steps.what: `final String orderId` (:28) â†’ `List<String> _orders` + `capacityUnits` + `loadedUnits` + `acceptingLoad`. The truck is structurally incapable of carrying a second order today, so 'depart when full' degenerates to 'depart when my one unit is loaded'. Generator assigns an order to an existing accepting truck with room and spawns a new truck only when none exists. Departure gate (:84-102) becomes `isFull || (dwellTicks > kMaxDwellTicks && loadedUnits > 0)` instead of `order.isSatisfied` â€” the dwell timeout is REQUIRED, else a half-full truck holds its bay forever and wipCap stalls the loop. Wire the real capacity data at packaging_constants.dart:36-83 (kTruckCapacity S/M/L/XL is fully defined and referenced by ZERO files outside its own barrel re-export), or a simpler kOutboundTruckCapacityUnits. Mint the never-minted JobKind.departTruck (job_board.dart:143) or delete it.
steps.risk: BREAKS p4_outbound_loop_test â€” it asserts `whereType<OutboundTruckBrain>()` isEmpty after 220 ticks; a capacity-gated truck won't depart on one unit until the dwell timeout. Extend the tick budget or pin capacity to one unit in the fixture. Keep the existing kMaxSeekTicks abort (:31,:69-74, review HT-2) intact â€” step 11 copies it to the inbound side.

steps.title: Close the Order at DEPARTURE, not at load
steps.what: outbound_robot_brain.dart:124 `completeJob(progressUnits:)` reaches job_board.dart:392-395 and closes the Order the instant progress hits orderedUnits â€” while the truck is still parked in the bay. Departure is then a consequence, not the cause (the truck polls and sees isSatisfied at :87-90), so an order is reported shipped before the truck moves a cell, and sweepTerminal can delete the closed Order out from under the still-docked truck (which is why the defensive `order == null` branch exists). Make the load credit the truck's fill counter (step 8) and set OrderStatus.fulfilling; the truck calls closeOrder on ACTUAL departure. Fix the now-false header note at outbound_truck_brain.dart:7 ('Departure closing the order = shipped == loaded').
steps.risk: Changes when p4_outbound_loop_test's open-order assertion flips â€” it already asserts the order closes 'on departure' (its own stated reason), so this makes the test honest rather than breaking it. Guard sweepTerminal against pruning an order whose assigned truck is still docked.

steps.title: StockMonitor: deficit-sized manifest, real reorder point, stall timeout
steps.what: (a) `manifest: 1` (:65) is a hardcoded literal with no relationship to `orderedUnits: reorderUnits` (:57) â€” the truck side already loops `for (var i = 0; i < manifest; i++)` (inbound_truck_brain.dart:90-99) and is genuinely multi-pallet capable, but nothing ever passes >1. Size the manifest from the rack's ACTUAL DEFICIT, not from reorderUnits: a naive ceil(reorderUnits/kLoosePerPallet) is a no-op, because reorderUnits defaults to kLoosePerPallet = exactly one pallet. (b) This is a LATENT PERMANENT STALL, not a churn bug: one absorbed pallet credits exactly 48 (putaway_robot_brain.dart:144-145) which happens to equal the default reorderUnits, so isSatisfied fires only by coincidence of two independently-declared constants being equal. Set reorderUnits=96 and progress caps at 48, the Order never satisfies, inFlight() (:40-43) blocks that SKU's trucks forever, and the one truck has already departed and deregistered. (c) Trigger on step 3's configurable reorder point. (d) React to unfulfillable outbound demand for a SKU with no rack stock â€” today the monitor is closed over rack contents (:47-52) exactly like the generator, so it cannot even perceive demand for an unstocked SKU. (e) Add an open-order timeout so a departed truck can't wedge a SKU permanently: Order.createdTick (job_board.dart:97) is written at :268 and READ NOWHERE, so no age-based expiry exists today.
steps.risk: p3_inbound_loop_test asserts the close path via _lowAnywhere; deficit-sized manifests change the pallet count per truck. Keep the `inFlight` open-Order debounce â€” it is what prevents truck churn today.

steps.title: InboundTruck: real cargo, arbitrated stepping, seek timeout, truck-adjacent unload
steps.what: Four coupled truck defects. (a) CARGO IS FICTIONAL: the truck has no inventory field (only skuId + int manifest, :34-36) and pickFromTruck (action_applier.dart:104-108) only loads the robot's cargo â€” no source read, no decrement, no empty check, void return. Contrast its siblings pickFromRack (:120-133, clamps) and takeFromStage (:144-151, returns false on empty). Add the designed `truckManifestProvider` (Map<truckId,int>) and make pickFromTruck return bool + decrement + refuse when empty; the IR resolves truckId from Job.subjectUnitId. (b) TRUCKS BYPASS THE ARBITER AND DESYNC IT: `_advance` calls bare moveTo (:126-133) with no reservation check, replan, or blocked counter â€” unlike all 4 robot brains (identical replan-at-4 / side-step-at-8 shape). Worse, unit_scheduler.dart:47-53 seeds cellReservationProvider with EVERY unit's cell including trucks, and moveTo never updates it â€” so a moving truck leaves a phantom reservation on its origin (blocking robots from an empty cell) while its real cell stays unreserved (letting a robot walk into the truck). Switch to tryStep + blocked counter. Same for outbound_truck_brain.dart:114. (c) NO-BAY WEDGE: with no dock cell, _bayCells is empty, perceiveAndDecide returns (:54-56), and the truck sits in seekingBay forever with no timeout â€” copy the sibling's `kMaxSeekTicks`/closeOrder(aborted:true)/remove (outbound_truck_brain.dart:31,69-74, the HT-2 fix the inbound side never got). (d) NEAR THE TRUCK: publish the truck's own pos on the unload job (src is currently the bay cell at :93, but the truck parks on _adjacentDriveable OF the bay) so the IR approaches the truck, not the bay â€” today both sit on neighbours of the bay and the IR's free-side preference DETERMINISTICALLY skips the truck's cell.
steps.risk: BREAKS p2_unload_chain_test â€” it mints an unloadTruck job at a dock cell with NO truck registered and passes; the IR unloads a truck that does not exist. Once pickFromTruck checks cargo, that fixture must register a truck (or seed truckManifestProvider). Also note _hasOutstandingUnloads (:120-124) filters kind/subjectUnitId with NO status filter and completeJob only sets status=done (never removes) â€” verify the sweeper actually runs, or the truck never departs.

steps.title: Landing area: the truck-side buffer (spec 4)
steps.what: There is NO landing concept anywhere today (a repo-wide grep for 'landing' hits one unrelated markdown row). The IR FSM is a SINGLE hop truckâ†’palletStaging: `_findStaging` hard-filters `if (c.type != CellType.palletStaging) continue` (:193) and the drop lands in stagingPalletsProvider. Retarget _findStaging to CellType.landing (step 2) with a palletStaging fallback for legacy layouts, and mint the follow-on putaway job FROM landing. Crucially, spec 4 needs BOTH halves: IR unloadsâ†’landing AND OR picks from landing. Today those are two DISJOINT buffers â€” IR drops to palletStaging (inbound-only) while OR takes from packStation via outboundStageProvider (outbound-only) â€” so no renaming can satisfy the spec; a shared truck-side buffer must actually exist. Reuse stagingPalletsProvider keyed to landing cells rather than adding a third provider.
steps.risk: p2_unload_chain_test and p1_putaway_test build grids with palletStaging cells â€” the fallback keeps them green. Without the fallback both break.

steps.title: Putaway 5.1: cross-dock to outbound staging (the never-ported rule)
steps.what: `_decideDestination(WarehouseConfig cfg, String sku)` (:226-227) takes only the static grid and a SKU â€” it is STRUCTURALLY INCAPABLE of reading an Order, which is why rule 5.1 was silently dropped in the port despite the doc comments at :4, :53 and :224 (and p1_putaway_test.dart:2) all claiming '5.1-5.4'. Change the signature to take BrainContext, add rule 5.1 (open outbound order for this SKU with unreserved demand â†’ free outboundStage cell, mirroring pick_robot_brain.dart:290-294 _findStage), and branch `_PR.dropping` (:134-148) to applier.stageOutbound instead of dropToRack. Add `outboundDemandReservationProvider` (Map<skuId,int>) alongside rackReservationProvider in bay_resource.dart so putaway-5.1 and PickRobot cannot both serve the same line (SC-5). Call the DEAD `Order.divert()` hook (job_board.dart:128-131 â€” defined, feeds remainingUnits, zero callers) and credit the inbound line via Job.divertedFromInboundLineId (step 5). Fix the four stale doc comments. Port the legacy twin's logic from pallet_putaway_controller.dart:407-419 but NOT its trigger: _hasOrderPending (:458) inspects static config.truckSpawns cargo, not real orders â€” its own comment concedes 'in real implementation this would query backend orders'.
steps.risk: Medium. The legacy PalletPutawayController is NOT ticked (robot_scout_simulation.dart:378-386 â€” it stays instantiated only for manual D-pad mode), so this is a genuine port, not a refactor. The SC-5 double-serve it guards against is currently unreachable precisely because 5.1 doesn't exist â€” the reservation provider must land WITH the rule, not after.

steps.title: PickRobot: honour job.qtyUnits, order-affine staging, zone binding, nearest-first
steps.what: (a) QUANTITY â€” the hard blocker for spec 1. Line :136 hardcodes `pickFromRack(this, _rack!, _sku, 1)` and :177 completes the job unconditionally; job.qtyUnits (job_board.dart:186) is NEVER READ by this brain (it only WRITES it at :175 for the packAndLoad handoff). Loop the FSM until qtyUnits is satisfied (or pick min(remaining, faceQty) per trip). This is non-optional before step 7: because completeJob passes no progressUnits, job_board.dart:392 won't advance the Order, so a 144-unit order does not under-deliver â€” it WEDGES at 48/144 with no job to mint the remainder, the truck holding its bay forever on isSatisfied, and wipCap blocking every future order. The releaseOrFail watchdog cannot rescue it (the job already settled `done`). (b) GROUPING: `_findStage` (:290-301) filters only on type/free/reserved â€” orderId is never consulted despite _orderId being held at :39. Prefer a cell already holding the same orderId, then nearest to order.shipBay. (c) ZONES (spec 5a/5b/5c): bind config.zoneForCell (warehouse_config.dart:539-551) in _findSource so a picker's handledUom is constrained to its PickZone. The three pick areas ALREADY exist as PickZoneType.pallet/casePick/loosePick + PickZoneDef, are painted by the template factory (:416/428/440), are paintable in the creator, and are consumed by exactly 3 UI sites (two tooltips + a render tint) â€” zero hits across lib/application. Bind the existing zones; do NOT resurrect looseStaging/caseStaging CellTypes, which would duplicate this model. (d) SHORTEST PATH (spec 8): legs are A*-optimal but ENDPOINT SELECTION is not â€” _findSource (:277-288) and _findStage both return the first match in cell order and don't even take `pos` as a parameter. Rank candidates by path length.
steps.risk: Highest-value single step; gates step 7. Preserve the existing AC-4/F2 ordering invariant (secure stage + path BEFORE consuming the idem key or decrementing the rack, :114-139) â€” a multi-trip loop must re-establish it per trip, and the idemKey must become per-trip (`:L$i:$trip`) or the second trip's rack decrement is rejected by consumeIdem as a duplicate. Keep the reserved-vs-no-stock distinction at :74-78 (release vs releaseOrFail).

steps.title: OutboundRobot: wait for the order's group, load into the pooled truck
steps.what: The claim gate is currently just `if (stage == null || shipBay == null) continue;` (:41-69) â€” no group-completeness wait, so it hauls each staged item individually to the truck the instant it appears. Gate on Order.isFullyStaged (step 5) so all lines are consolidated before loading â€” this is spec 2's 'GROUPED TOGETHER per order'. Load into the pooled truck's fill counter (step 8) rather than closing the order (step 9). Per spec 4, the OR should source from the landing/shipping location (step 12).
steps.risk: Low. Note the grouping failure is currently UNREACHABLE (one UOM-unit per order â‡’ one stage cell â‡’ grouping vacuously satisfied); it becomes live the moment step 7's multi-line orders land, which is why this must precede/accompany step 7.

steps.title: Charging: mid-job preemption, arbitrated charge route, nearest reachable charger
steps.what: Spec 9's literal behaviour IS implemented for IDLE robots (CAS charger arbiter, A* route, 20/60 hysteresis, offline recovery â€” p5_charging_test passes). Three gaps remain. (a) MID-JOB: startChargeIfNeeded bails on `!isIdle` (:137), and isIdle == (currentJobId == null && lifecycle == idle) (:91), so a robot that goes low WHILE working never diverts â€” it works to battery 0 and is force-dropped offline (unit_scheduler.dart:40-42), aborting its Job and shedding cargo. Drop the isIdle gate and add suspend+resume: use plain `release` (not releaseOrFail) so a charge break doesn't burn an attempt toward the kMaxJobAttempts=8 abort. The in-file comment at :107 concedes this. (b) ARBITER BYPASS: chargeStep advances with bare `moveTo` (:162) while every other robot movement uses tryStep â€” cellReservationProvider is read in exactly ONE place in the codebase (action_applier.dart:48). A charge-seeking robot walks through reserved cells AND leaves a phantom reservation behind. Switch to tryStep and only advance _chargeIdx on success (it currently increments unconditionally at :161, so a naive fix desyncs the index from the true position). (c) CHARGER CHOICE: _chargerCells (:183-186) + claimFirstFree take the first charger in cell order, not the nearest. Worse, the A* path is computed AFTER the claim (:143-147) â€” if that charger is unreachable the code releases and returns false, and next tick claimFirstFree deterministically returns the SAME unreachable charger: a genuine livelock. Score candidates by distance and fall through to the next on failure. Also revisit the low-battery patrol freeze (:251), which leaves a robot that can't get a charger neither charging nor patrolling.
